preview.tournamentOverview: TPC Twin Cities is a parkland layout that places a premium on approach play and total strokes gained. The 3M Open offers a strong field and solid betting markets, making it a key mid-summer stop for both outright and placement wagers.
preview.modelExplainer: SG Approach (25.8%) and SG Total (22.8%) dominate the model, followed by SG Putting (16.8%) and SG Around the Green (13.3%). Driving Accuracy (3.9%) and Bogey Avoidance (3.0%) carry lighter weight, so the model prioritizes players who gain strokes on approach and avoid big numbers over pure distance.
preview.pickApproach: Top-tier bets target golfers inside the model top 10 who also rank well in SG Approach and SG Total. Mid-tier wagers look at players ranked 11-30 who show strong approach numbers, while long-shot bets focus on outliers with elite SG Putting or SG Around the Green that the model undervalues.

article.title: 3M Open Model Targets at TPC Twin Cities
article.dek: Model ranks place Jake Knapp first and Scottie Scheffler second, with several players showing large gaps between tournament rank and power rank.
article.introduction: The tournament model weights approach play at 25.8% and off-the-tee at 11.4%, producing a top-20 list led by Jake Knapp at rank 1 with sgTotal 2.002. Scottie Scheffler sits at model rank 2 with sgTotal 1.868, while Hideki Matsuyama, David Lipsky, and Maverick McNealy occupy ranks 4-6. The field contains 144 players at TPC Twin Cities starting 2026-07-23.

sections.heading: Tournament Overview
sections.body: The 144-player 3M Open at TPC Twin Cities carries course weights of SG APP 25.8%, SG OTT 11.4%, SG PUT 16.8%, and SG ATG 13.3%. The model ranks Jake Knapp first and Scottie Scheffler second on sgTotal marks of 2.002 and 1.868.

sections.heading: Course Fit
sections.body: Approach play at 25.8% weight favors players such as Austin Smotherman (sgApp 0.874) and Kurt Kitayama (sgApp 0.728), while off-the-tee weight of 11.4% penalizes negative sgOTT figures like Andrew Putnam (-0.341) and David Lipsky (-0.31).

sections.heading: Model Strength vs Power Rank
sections.body: Jake Knapp leads both model rank 1 and power rank 1, while Scottie Scheffler holds model rank 2 and power rank 2. Larger gaps appear for Kurt Kitayama (model 11 vs power 67), Gary Woodland (model 14 vs power 46), and Beau Hossler (model 19 vs power 49).

sections.heading: Outright Targets
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler (model rank 2) carries sgApp 0.08, the weakest approach mark among the top-five model ranks. Hideki Matsuyama (model rank 4) shows sgOTT -0.099, the weakest off-the-tee figure in that group. David Lipsky (model rank 5) posts sgOTT -0.31, the lowest off-the-tee number among the top-five ranks.

sections.heading: Top-5 Targets
sections.body: Scottie Scheffler, Hideki Matsuyama, David Lipsky, Maverick McNealy, and Andrew Putnam occupy model ranks 2, 4, 5, 6, and 8. Each carries at least one negative or below-group sgOTT or sgApp value that conflicts with the 11.4% or 25.8% course weights.

sections.heading: Top-10 Targets
sections.body: Jake Knapp (model rank 1) leads with sgPutt 1.285 but faces putting regression risk after a four-round workload. Scottie Scheffler (model rank 2) and Hideki Matsuyama (model rank 4) follow, while Rasmus Højgaard (model rank 10) shows sgAtG -0.273 and Kurt Kitayama (model rank 11) shows sgPutt -0.227.

sections.heading: Top-20 Targets
sections.body: David Lipsky (model rank 5), Maverick McNealy (model rank 6), Andrew Putnam (model rank 8), and Sam Stevens (model rank 9) sit inside the top-10 model ranks. Gary Woodland (model rank 14), Christiaan Bezuidenhout (model rank 15), and Beau Hossler (model rank 19) extend the list with sgAtG or sgApp marks below zero.

sections.heading: Crossover Research Angles
sections.body: Hideki Matsuyama and David Lipsky each carry a top-10 model rank after skipping the Scottish Open. Maverick McNealy and Sam Stevens combine a missed Open cut with limited tracked reps yet still rank inside the top-10 model list. Kurt Kitayama enters with an eight-round workload and safe FedExCup position at rank 32.

sections.heading: Data Limitations
sections.body: Weather and tee-time data are unavailable. Workload coverage includes only recorded rounds from The Open Championship and Genesis Scottish Open; opposite-field, DP World Tour, LIV, and other events may be missing, so no tracked rounds does not necessarily mean the player rested.

sections.heading: Final Betting Card
sections.body: Strong model-supported tier: Jake Knapp and Scottie Scheffler. Secondary tier: Hideki Matsuyama, David Lipsky, Maverick McNealy, Andrew Putnam, Rasmus Højgaard, Kurt Kitayama. Speculative tier: Sam Stevens, Gary Woodland, Christiaan Bezuidenhout, Beau Hossler.

article.conclusion: Primary model targets are Jake Knapp (rank 1) and Scottie Scheffler (rank 2); secondary model targets include Hideki Matsuyama (rank 4), David Lipsky (rank 5), and Maverick McNealy (rank 6).

keyTakeaways.text: Jake Knapp leads the model at rank 1 with sgTotal 2.002 and sgPutt 1.285, but his four-round workload and Open miss create a regression risk on putting.
Scottie Scheffler ranks second with sgTotal 1.868 yet posts the lowest sgApp 0.08 among the top-five model ranks, limiting upside if approach play stalls.
Hideki Matsuyama, David Lipsky, and Maverick McNealy sit inside the top-six model ranks despite sgOTT marks of -0.099, -0.31, and 0.31 respectively.
Andrew Putnam, Sam Stevens, and Rasmus Højgaard each post tournament ranks inside the top-10 while their power ranks sit between 29 and 45.
Kurt Kitayama and Gary Woodland show model ranks of 11 and 14 against power ranks of 67 and 46, highlighting the largest rank differentials in the top-20.

methodologyNotes: Picks are generated from a course-weighted strokes-gained model, cross-checked against the official PGA TOUR field.
Post-Open Championship, Scottish Open, and FedExCup context is included because this is the tournament immediately following The Open Championship.
dataLimitations: Weather and tee-time data are not available in this pipeline; the article does not make weather- or tee-time-specific claims.

Workload coverage includes only recorded rounds from The Open Championship and Genesis Scottish Open. Opposite-field, DP World Tour, LIV, and other events may be missing, so “No tracked rounds” does not necessarily mean the player rested.
